measures.title: Graduate futures pack framework passes approval and pilot gates
measures.questionAnswered: Has the graduate futures pack framework, including its data measures, representations and self-run workshop, been judged useful, credible and suitable for wider course-level use?
measures.measure: Completion of three gates: the project Reference Group approves the usefulness and responsible presentation of the measures; Education Executive approves the framework as proportionate and suitable for course and department use; and the pilot shows that teams can interpret the pack, complete the workshop and reach a meaningful course-level review.
measures.measureType: readiness, governance approval and user acceptance
measures.owner: Daniel Robson, with each approving body responsible for its respective gate
measures.cadence: Once during initial framework development and again after any material framework change
measures.baseline: The framework has not yet completed a formal reference, institutional approval and pilot acceptance process.
measures.target: Reference Group approval and Education Executive approval are recorded; pilot acceptance criteria are met; material issues and approval conditions are resolved or formally accepted; and the framework is authorised for wider production and distribution.
measures.dataSource: Reference Group decision record, Education Executive decision or minutes, pilot evaluation returns, pilot review record and issue-resolution log.

measures.title: Course pack production and distribution coverage
measures.questionAnswered: How completely has the approved graduate futures pack framework reached the agreed population of courses?
measures.measure: Count and percentage of in-scope courses for which a course-level pack has been checked and distributed, with the self-run workshop materials, to a named course or department contact. Report production and distribution separately for the first and second rollouts, and by department and faculty. Packs may be sent as departmental bundles, but coverage is counted by course.
measures.owner: Daniel Robson, supported by Careers and Employability Service, data colleagues and faculty or department contacts
measures.cadence: Monthly during active production, with formal reporting at the end of each rollout phase
measures.baseline: No course packs have yet been produced and distributed through the approved framework.
measures.target: After the pilot, every in-scope course in the first rollout, covering the agreed focal areas, receives a checked pack before its review window. Every other in-scope course receives one in the second rollout. Any exception records the reason, owner and agreed resolution.
measures.dataSource: Agreed course population, pack production register, quality-assurance record and distribution log.

measures.title: Graduate Futures Claims and Actions on a Page completed and confirmed
measures.questionAnswered: Are course teams able to turn their graduate futures evidence and course-level review into a concise, course-owned account of the graduate futures their course enables?
measures.measure: Count and percentage of in-scope courses that complete the first side of a Graduate Futures Claim and Action on a Page and confirm that it is fair, useful, evidence-informed and appropriately caveated. To count, it must include the central narrative, supporting evidence, the course's distinctive contribution, relevant caveats, course confirmation and its status for wider use.
measures.owner: Heads of Department, with course-level completion led by course leads and central collation by Daniel Robson
measures.cadence: At completion of each course review, reported termly and synthesised at the end of each rollout phase
measures.baseline: No in-scope courses have completed and confirmed a Graduate Futures Claim and Action on a Page through the approved framework.
measures.target: Every focal course completes and confirms the first side during the first rollout. Every remaining in-scope course does so during the second rollout, apart from documented and approved exceptions. Every claim counted meets the agreed completion standard.
measures.dataSource: Completed Graduate Futures Claim and Action on a Page, course confirmation record and central course-review register.

measures.title: Course reviews reach a clear graduate futures conclusion
measures.questionAnswered: Are course teams using the evidence to reach a clear conclusion about whether any action is needed?
measures.measure: Count and percentage of completed Graduate Futures Claim and Action on a Page templates whose second side records a strength to amplify, an enhancement action, a question requiring further investigation, or a reasoned conclusion that no additional action is required. Any proposed action or investigation records what will be done, why, how it could support students' graduate futures and where it will be taken forward.
measures.measureType: qualitative benefit realisation and decision quality
measures.owner: Course leads and Heads of Department, with faculty education leads supporting consistent interpretation and central synthesis led by Daniel Robson
measures.cadence: At completion of each course review, reported termly and synthesised at the end of each rollout phase
measures.baseline: No course-level actionable conclusions have yet been recorded through the approved framework.
measures.target: Every completed review records a clear conclusion supported by evidence or rationale. Every proposed action explains its intended contribution to students' graduate futures. There is no numerical target for how many courses should identify an enhancement.
measures.dataSource: Completed second side of the Graduate Futures Claim and Action on a Page and the central course-review register.

measures.title: Graduate futures actions enter existing quality enhancement monitoring
measures.questionAnswered: Are agreed graduate futures actions given an owner and added to the appropriate existing quality enhancement process?
measures.measure: For every agreed action, record whether it has a named owner, an appropriate course, department or faculty route, a timescale or review date, and a place in the relevant existing quality enhancement process. Report both how many actions have entered monitoring and how many courses have at least one action in monitoring. Courses concluding that no action is required are reported separately.
measures.owner: Academic Standards and Quality Enhancement Subcommittee of Education Committee for institutional oversight, with course leads, Heads of Department and faculty education leads responsible for local incorporation
measures.cadence: Aligned with existing quality enhancement monitoring cycles and the Subcommittee's established reporting timetable
measures.baseline: No graduate futures actions have yet been incorporated into existing quality enhancement monitoring through this framework.
measures.target: Every agreed action has a named owner and review date and enters the appropriate existing quality enhancement process. If an action cannot enter that process, record the reason and agree another route.
measures.dataSource: Graduate Futures Claim and Action on a Page, existing course and department quality enhancement records, relevant monitoring returns, and Subcommittee papers or records.

measures.title: Graduate futures actions progressed through quality enhancement processes
measures.questionAnswered: Are agreed graduate futures actions being progressed and kept up to date through existing quality enhancement processes?
measures.measure: For each action that has reached its review date, record whether it is underway and on track, completed, revised, closed with a reason, overdue or needing escalation, or missing an update. Also report how many courses have at least one action underway or completed, the recurring themes and any actions that need institutional support.
measures.owner: Academic Standards and Quality Enhancement Subcommittee of Education Committee for institutional oversight, with named action owners and relevant course, department or faculty leads responsible for delivery and status updates
measures.cadence: At existing quality enhancement review points, following the Subcommittee's normal reporting timetable
measures.baseline: No graduate futures actions have yet reached a formal monitoring point through this framework.
measures.target: Every action due for review has a current status. Completed actions have proportionate evidence; revised or closed actions have a recorded reason; overdue actions have a recovery, support or escalation route; and recurring issues are visible to the appropriate faculty or institutional body.
measures.dataSource: Existing course, department and faculty quality enhancement action records, scheduled monitoring returns, and relevant Subcommittee papers or records.
